ddls:
CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the project (e.g., Active, Completed) */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project manager responsible for the project */,
  "budget" TEXT /* Total budget allocated for the project */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client for whom the project is being developed */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the project (e.g., 1 for high, 2 for medium, 3 for low) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the project was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the project was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "milestones" (
  "milestone_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each milestone */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the project to which the milestone belongs */,
  "milestone_name" TEXT /* Name or description of the milestone */,
  "scheduled_date" TEXT /* Scheduled date for milestone completion */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the team member assigned to the milestone */,
  "modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the milestone was last modified */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments regarding the milestone's progress or issues */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the status of the milestone */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the milestone */,
  "estimated_cost" TEXT /* Estimated cost for completing the milestone */,
  "actual_cost" TEXT /* Actual cost incurred for completing the milestone */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the milestone was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the milestone was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("milestone_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_milestones_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_milestones_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES project_status ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_milestones_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., project manager, developer) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "project_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., Active, Completed) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the status represents */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the status was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the status was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "milestone_id" INTEGER /* ID of the milestone the comment is related to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date the comment was made */,
  "parent_comment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent comment (for nested comments) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the comment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the comment was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_parent_comment_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_comment_id") REFERENCES comments ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_milestone_id FOREIGN KEY ("milestone_id") REFERENCES milestones ("milestone_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "task_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each task assignment */,
  "milestone_id" INTEGER /* ID of the milestone the task is related to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user assigned to the task */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Due date for the task completion */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the task (e.g., In Progress, Completed) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the task assignment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the task assignment was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_assignments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_assignments_milestone_id FOREIGN KEY ("milestone_id") REFERENCES milestones ("milestone_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attachments" (
  "attachment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attachment */,
  "milestone_id" INTEGER /* ID of the milestone the attachment belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* Path to the uploaded file */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the file (e.g., PDF, DOCX) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the file */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the attachment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the attachment was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attachment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attachments_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attachments_milestone_id FOREIGN KEY ("milestone_id") REFERENCES milestones ("milestone_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the notification is sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification message */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., Unread, Read) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the notification was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the notification was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
